ayahs.textArabic: حمٓ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hameem
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Ha, Meem.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Ḥâ-Mĩm.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلۡكِتَٰبِ ٱلۡمُبِينِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waalkitabi almubeeni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: By the clear Book,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: By the clear Book!

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّا جَعَلۡنَٰهُ قُرۡءَٰنًا عَرَبِيّٗا لَّعَلَّكُمۡ تَعۡقِلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna jaAAalnahu quranan AAarabiyyan laAAallakum taAAqiloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, We have made it an Arabic Qur'an that you might understand.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Certainly, We have made it a Quran in Arabic so perhaps you will understand.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِنَّهُۥ فِيٓ أُمِّ ٱلۡكِتَٰبِ لَدَيۡنَا لَعَلِيٌّ حَكِيمٌ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wainnahu fee ommi alkitabi ladayna laAAaliyyun hakeemun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed it is, in the Mother of the Book with Us, exalted and full of wisdom.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And indeed, it is—in the Master Record with Us—highly esteemed, rich in wisdom.

ayahs.textArabic: أَفَنَضۡرِبُ عَنكُمُ ٱلذِّكۡرَ صَفۡحًا أَن كُنتُمۡ قَوۡمٗا مُّسۡرِفِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Afanadribu AAankumu alththikra safhan an kuntum qawman musrifeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then should We turn the message away, disregarding you, because you are a transgressing people?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Should We then turn the ˹Quranic˺ Reminder away from you ˹simply˺ because you have been a transgressing people?

ayahs.textArabic: وَكَمۡ أَرۡسَلۡنَا مِن نَّبِيّٖ فِي ٱلۡأَوَّلِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wakam arsalna min nabiyyin fee alawwaleena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And how many a prophet We sent among the former peoples,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Imagine˺ how many prophets We sent to those ˹destroyed˺ before!

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا يَأۡتِيهِم مِّن نَّبِيٍّ إِلَّا كَانُواْ بِهِۦ يَسۡتَهۡزِءُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama yateehim min nabiyyin illa kanoo bihi yastahzioona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But there would not come to them a prophet except that they used to ridicule him.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But no prophet ever came to them without being mocked.

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَهۡلَكۡنَآ أَشَدَّ مِنۡهُم بَطۡشٗا وَمَضَىٰ مَثَلُ ٱلۡأَوَّلِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faahlakna ashadda minhum batshan wamada mathalu alawwaleena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We destroyed greater than them in [striking] power, and the example of the former peoples has preceded.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So We destroyed those who were far mightier than these ˹Meccans˺. The examples of ˹their˺ predecessors have ˹already˺ been related.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَئِن سَأَلۡتَهُم مَّنۡ خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضَ لَيَقُولُنَّ خَلَقَهُنَّ ٱلۡعَزِيزُ ٱلۡعَلِيمُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walain saaltahum man khalaqa alssamawati waalarda layaqoolunna khalaqahunna alAAazeezu alAAaleemu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And if you should ask them, "Who has created the heavens and the earth?" they would surely say, "They were created by the Exalted in Might, the Knowing."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: If you ask them ˹O Prophet˺ who created the heavens and the earth, they will certainly say, “The Almighty, All-Knowing did.”

ayahs.textArabic: ٱلَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلۡأَرۡضَ مَهۡدٗا وَجَعَلَ لَكُمۡ فِيهَا سُبُلٗا لَّعَلَّكُمۡ تَهۡتَدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Allathee jaAAala lakumu alarda mahdan wajaAAala lakum feeha subulan laAAallakum tahtadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [The one] who has made for you the earth a bed and made for you upon it roads that you might be guided
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹He is the One˺ Who has laid out the earth for you, and set in it pathways for you so that you may find your way.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّذِي نَزَّلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءَۢ بِقَدَرٖ فَأَنشَرۡنَا بِهِۦ بَلۡدَةٗ مَّيۡتٗاۚ كَذَٰلِكَ تُخۡرَجُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallathee nazzala mina alssamai maan biqadarin faansharna bihi baldatan maytan kathalika tukhrajoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And who sends down rain from the sky in measured amounts, and We revive thereby a dead land - thus will you be brought forth -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And ˹He is the One˺ Who sends down rain from the sky in perfect measure, with which We give life to a lifeless land. And so will you be brought forth ˹from the grave˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّذِي خَلَقَ ٱلۡأَزۡوَٰجَ كُلَّهَا وَجَعَلَ لَكُم مِّنَ ٱلۡفُلۡكِ وَٱلۡأَنۡعَٰمِ مَا تَرۡكَبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallathee khalaqa alazwaja kullaha wajaAAala lakum mina alfulki waalanAAami ma tarkaboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And who created the species, all of them, and has made for you of ships and animals those which you mount.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And ˹He is the One˺ Who created all ˹things in˺ pairs, and made for you ships and animals to ride

ayahs.textArabic: لِتَسۡتَوُۥاْ عَلَىٰ ظُهُورِهِۦ ثُمَّ تَذۡكُرُواْ نِعۡمَةَ رَبِّكُمۡ إِذَا ٱسۡتَوَيۡتُمۡ عَلَيۡهِ وَتَقُولُواْ سُبۡحَٰنَ ٱلَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَنَا هَٰذَا وَمَا كُنَّا لَهُۥ مُقۡرِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Litastawoo AAala thuhoorihi thumma tathkuroo niAAmata rabbikum itha istawaytum AAalayhi wataqooloo subhana allathee sakhkhara lana hatha wama kunna lahu muqrineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That you may settle yourselves upon their backs and then remember the favor of your Lord when you have settled upon them and say. "Exalted is He who has subjected this to us, and we could not have [otherwise] subdued it.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: so that you may sit firmly on their backs, and remember your Lord’s blessings once you are settled on them, saying, “Glory be to the One Who has subjected these for us, for we could have never done so ˹on our own˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِنَّآ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا لَمُنقَلِبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wainna ila rabbina lamunqaliboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed we, to our Lord, will [surely] return."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And surely to our Lord we will ˹all˺ return.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَجَعَلُواْ لَهُۥ مِنۡ عِبَادِهِۦ جُزۡءًاۚ إِنَّ ٱلۡإِنسَٰنَ لَكَفُورٞ مُّبِينٌ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WajaAAaloo lahu min AAibadihi juzan inna alinsana lakafoorun mubeenun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But they have attributed to Him from His servants a portion. Indeed, man is clearly ungrateful.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Still the pagans have made some of His creation out to be a part of Him. Indeed, humankind is clearly ungrateful.

ayahs.textArabic: أَمِ ٱتَّخَذَ مِمَّا يَخۡلُقُ بَنَاتٖ وَأَصۡفَىٰكُم بِٱلۡبَنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ami ittakhatha mimma yakhluqu banatin waasfakum bialbaneena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or has He taken, out of what He has created, daughters and chosen you for [having] sons?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Has He taken ˹angels as His˺ daughters from what He created, and favoured you ˹O pagans˺ with sons?

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِذَا بُشِّرَ أَحَدُهُم بِمَا ضَرَبَ لِلرَّحۡمَٰنِ مَثَلٗا ظَلَّ وَجۡهُهُۥ مُسۡوَدّٗا وَهُوَ كَظِيمٌ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waitha bushshira ahaduhum bima daraba lilrrahmani mathalan thalla wajhuhu muswaddan wahuwa katheemun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when one of them is given good tidings of that which he attributes to the Most Merciful in comparison, his face becomes dark, and he suppresses grief.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Whenever one of them is given the good news of what they attribute to the Most Compassionate, his face grows gloomy, as he suppresses his rage.

ayahs.textArabic: أَوَمَن يُنَشَّؤُاْ فِي ٱلۡحِلۡيَةِ وَهُوَ فِي ٱلۡخِصَامِ غَيۡرُ مُبِينٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Awaman yunashshao fee alhilyati wahuwa fee alkhisami ghayru mubeenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So is one brought up in ornaments while being during conflict unevident [attributed to Allah]?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Do they attribute to Him˺ those who are brought up in fineries and are not commanding in disputes?

ayahs.textArabic: وَجَعَلُواْ ٱلۡمَلَٰٓئِكَةَ ٱلَّذِينَ هُمۡ عِبَٰدُ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ إِنَٰثًاۚ أَشَهِدُواْ خَلۡقَهُمۡۚ سَتُكۡتَبُ شَهَٰدَتُهُمۡ وَيُسۡـَٔلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WajaAAaloo almalaikata allatheena hum AAibadu alrrahmani inathan ashahidoo khalqahum satuktabu shahadatuhum wayusaloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they have made the angels, who are servants of the Most Merciful, females. Did they witness their creation? Their testimony will be recorded, and they will be questioned.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Still they have labelled the angels, who are servants of the Most Compassionate, as female. Did they witness their creation? Their statement will be recorded, and they will be questioned!

ayahs.textArabic: وَقَالُواْ لَوۡ شَآءَ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ مَا عَبَدۡنَٰهُمۗ مَّا لَهُم بِذَٰلِكَ مِنۡ عِلۡمٍۖ إِنۡ هُمۡ إِلَّا يَخۡرُصُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqaloo law shaa alrrahmanu ma AAabadnahum ma lahum bithalika min AAilmin in hum illa yakhrusoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they said, "If the Most Merciful had willed, we would not have worshipped them." They have of that no knowledge. They are not but falsifying.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And they argue, “Had the Most Compassionate willed, we would have never worshipped them.” They have no knowledge ˹in support˺ of this ˹claim˺. They do nothing but lie.

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ ءَاتَيۡنَٰهُمۡ كِتَٰبٗا مِّن قَبۡلِهِۦ فَهُم بِهِۦ مُسۡتَمۡسِكُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am ataynahum kitaban min qablihi fahum bihi mustamsikoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or have We given them a book before the Qur'an to which they are adhering?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or have We given them a Book ˹for proof˺, before this ˹Quran˺, to which they are holding firm?

ayahs.textArabic: بَلۡ قَالُوٓاْ إِنَّا وَجَدۡنَآ ءَابَآءَنَا عَلَىٰٓ أُمَّةٖ وَإِنَّا عَلَىٰٓ ءَاثَٰرِهِم مُّهۡتَدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Bal qaloo inna wajadna abaana AAala ommatin wainna AAala atharihim muhtadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Rather, they say, "Indeed, we found our fathers upon a religion, and we are in their footsteps [rightly] guided."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In fact, they say, “We found our forefathers following a ˹particular˺ way, and we are following in their footsteps.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَكَذَٰلِكَ مَآ أَرۡسَلۡنَا مِن قَبۡلِكَ فِي قَرۡيَةٖ مِّن نَّذِيرٍ إِلَّا قَالَ مُتۡرَفُوهَآ إِنَّا وَجَدۡنَآ ءَابَآءَنَا عَلَىٰٓ أُمَّةٖ وَإِنَّا عَلَىٰٓ ءَاثَٰرِهِم مُّقۡتَدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wakathalika ma arsalna min qablika fee qaryatin min natheerin illa qala mutrafooha inna wajadna abaana AAala ommatin wainna AAala atharihim muqtadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And similarly, We did not send before you any warner into a city except that its affluent said, "Indeed, we found our fathers upon a religion, and we are, in their footsteps, following."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Similarly, whenever We sent a warner to a society before you ˹O Prophet˺, its ˹spoiled˺ elite would say, “We found our forefathers following a ˹particular˺ way, and we are walking in their footsteps.”

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ قَٰلَ أَوَلَوۡ جِئۡتُكُم بِأَهۡدَىٰ مِمَّا وَجَدتُّمۡ عَلَيۡهِ ءَابَآءَكُمۡۖ قَالُوٓاْ إِنَّا بِمَآ أُرۡسِلۡتُم بِهِۦ كَٰفِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala awalaw jitukum biahda mimma wajadtum AAalayhi abaakum qaloo inna bima orsiltum bihi kafiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Each warner] said, "Even if I brought you better guidance than that [religion] upon which you found your fathers?" They said, "Indeed we, in that with which you were sent, are disbelievers."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Each ˹warner˺ asked, “Even if what I brought you is better guidance than what you found your forefathers practicing?” They replied, “We totally reject whatever you have been sent with.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَٱنتَقَمۡنَا مِنۡهُمۡۖ فَٱنظُرۡ كَيۡفَ كَانَ عَٰقِبَةُ ٱلۡمُكَذِّبِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faintaqamna minhum faonthur kayfa kana AAaqibatu almukaththibeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So we took retribution from them; then see how was the end of the deniers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So We inflicted punishment upon them. See then what was the fate of the deniers!

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِذۡ قَالَ إِبۡرَٰهِيمُ لِأَبِيهِ وَقَوۡمِهِۦٓ إِنَّنِي بَرَآءٞ مِّمَّا تَعۡبُدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waith qala ibraheemu liabeehi waqawmihi innanee baraon mimma taAAbudoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [mention, O Muhammad], when Abraham said to his father and his people, "Indeed, I am disassociated from that which you worship
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Remember, O Prophet˺ when Abraham declared to his father and his people, “I am totally free of whatever ˹gods˺ you worship,

ayahs.textArabic: إِلَّا ٱلَّذِي فَطَرَنِي فَإِنَّهُۥ سَيَهۡدِينِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Illa allathee fataranee fainnahu sayahdeeni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Except for He who created me; and indeed, He will guide me."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: except the One Who originated me, and He will surely guide me.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَجَعَلَهَا كَلِمَةَۢ بَاقِيَةٗ فِي عَقِبِهِۦ لَعَلَّهُمۡ يَرۡجِعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WajaAAalaha kalimatan baqiyatan fee AAaqibihi laAAallahum yarjiAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And he made it a word remaining among his descendants that they might return [to it].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And he left this enduring declaration among his descendants, so they may ˹always˺ turn back ˹to Allah˺.

ayahs.textArabic: بَلۡ مَتَّعۡتُ هَٰٓؤُلَآءِ وَءَابَآءَهُمۡ حَتَّىٰ جَآءَهُمُ ٱلۡحَقُّ وَرَسُولٞ مُّبِينٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Bal mattaAAtu haolai waabaahum hatta jaahumu alhaqqu warasoolun mubeenun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: However, I gave enjoyment to these [people of Mecca] and their fathers until there came to them the truth and a clear Messenger.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In fact, I had allowed enjoyment for these ˹Meccans˺ and their forefathers, until the truth came to them along with a messenger making things clear.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَمَّا جَآءَهُمُ ٱلۡحَقُّ قَالُواْ هَٰذَا سِحۡرٞ وَإِنَّا بِهِۦ كَٰفِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walamma jaahumu alhaqqu qaloo hatha sihrun wainna bihi kafiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But when the truth came to them, they said, "This is magic, and indeed we are, concerning it, disbelievers."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹But˺ when the truth came to them, they said, “This is magic, and we totally reject it.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَقَالُواْ لَوۡلَا نُزِّلَ هَٰذَا ٱلۡقُرۡءَانُ عَلَىٰ رَجُلٖ مِّنَ ٱلۡقَرۡيَتَيۡنِ عَظِيمٍ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqaloo lawla nuzzila hatha alquranu AAala rajulin mina alqaryatayni AAatheemun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they said, "Why was this Qur'an not sent down upon a great man from [one of] the two cities?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And they exclaimed, “If only this Quran was revealed to a great man from ˹one of˺ the two cities!”

ayahs.textArabic: أَهُمۡ يَقۡسِمُونَ رَحۡمَتَ رَبِّكَۚ نَحۡنُ قَسَمۡنَا بَيۡنَهُم مَّعِيشَتَهُمۡ فِي ٱلۡحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنۡيَاۚ وَرَفَعۡنَا بَعۡضَهُمۡ فَوۡقَ بَعۡضٖ دَرَجَٰتٖ لِّيَتَّخِذَ بَعۡضُهُم بَعۡضٗا سُخۡرِيّٗاۗ وَرَحۡمَتُ رَبِّكَ خَيۡرٞ مِّمَّا يَجۡمَعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ahum yaqsimoona rahmata rabbika nahnu qasamna baynahum maAAeeshatahum fee alhayati alddunya warafaAAna baAAdahum fawqa baAAdin darajatin liyattakhitha baAAduhum baAAdan sukhriyyan warahmatu rabbika khayrun mimma yajmaAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Do they distribute the mercy of your Lord? It is We who have apportioned among them their livelihood in the life of this world and have raised some of them above others in degrees [of rank] that they may make use of one another for service. But the mercy of your Lord is better than whatever they accumulate.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Is it they who distribute your Lord’s mercy? We ˹alone˺ have distributed their ˹very˺ livelihood among them in this worldly life and raised some of them in rank above others so that some may employ others in service. ˹But˺ your Lord’s mercy is far better than whatever ˹wealth˺ they amass.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَوۡلَآ أَن يَكُونَ ٱلنَّاسُ أُمَّةٗ وَٰحِدَةٗ لَّجَعَلۡنَا لِمَن يَكۡفُرُ بِٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ لِبُيُوتِهِمۡ سُقُفٗا مِّن فِضَّةٖ وَمَعَارِجَ عَلَيۡهَا يَظۡهَرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walawla an yakoona alnnasu ommatan wahidatan lajaAAalna liman yakfuru bialrrahmani libuyootihim suqufan min fiddatin wamaAAarija AAalayha yathharoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And if it were not that the people would become one community [of disbelievers], We would have made for those who disbelieve in the Most Merciful - for their houses - ceilings and stairways of silver upon which to mount
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Were it not that people might ˹be tempted to˺ become one community ˹of disbelievers˺, We would have supplied the homes of ˹only˺ those who disbelieve in the Most Compassionate with silver roofs and ˹silver˺ stairways to ascend,

ayahs.textArabic: وَلِبُيُوتِهِمۡ أَبۡوَٰبٗا وَسُرُرًا عَلَيۡهَا يَتَّكِـُٔونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walibuyootihim abwaban wasururan AAalayha yattakioona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And for their houses - doors and couches [of silver] upon which to recline
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: as well as ˹silver˺ gates and thrones to recline on,

ayahs.textArabic: وَزُخۡرُفٗاۚ وَإِن كُلُّ ذَٰلِكَ لَمَّا مَتَٰعُ ٱلۡحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنۡيَاۚ وَٱلۡأٓخِرَةُ عِندَ رَبِّكَ لِلۡمُتَّقِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wazukhrufan wain kullu thalika lamma mataAAu alhayati alddunya waalakhiratu AAinda rabbika lilmuttaqeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And gold ornament. But all that is not but the enjoyment of worldly life. And the Hereafter with your Lord is for the righteous.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and ornaments ˹of gold˺. Yet all this is no more than a ˹fleeting˺ enjoyment in this worldly life. ˹But˺ the Hereafter with your Lord is ˹only˺ for those mindful ˹of Him˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَن يَعۡشُ عَن ذِكۡرِ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ نُقَيِّضۡ لَهُۥ شَيۡطَٰنٗا فَهُوَ لَهُۥ قَرِينٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waman yaAAshu AAan thikri alrrahmani nuqayyid lahu shaytanan fahuwa lahu qareenun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And whoever is blinded from remembrance of the Most Merciful - We appoint for him a devil, and he is to him a companion.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And whoever turns a blind eye to the Reminder of the Most Compassionate, We place at the disposal of each a devilish one as their close associate,

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِنَّهُمۡ لَيَصُدُّونَهُمۡ عَنِ ٱلسَّبِيلِ وَيَحۡسَبُونَ أَنَّهُم مُّهۡتَدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wainnahum layasuddoonahum AAani alssabeeli wayahsaboona annahum muhtadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, the devils avert them from the way [of guidance] while they think that they are [rightly] guided
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: who will certainly hinder them from the ˹Right˺ Way while they think they are ˹rightly˺ guided.

ayahs.textArabic: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءَنَا قَالَ يَٰلَيۡتَ بَيۡنِي وَبَيۡنَكَ بُعۡدَ ٱلۡمَشۡرِقَيۡنِ فَبِئۡسَ ٱلۡقَرِينُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hatta itha jaana qala ya layta baynee wabaynaka buAAda almashriqayni fabisa alqareenu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Until, when he comes to Us [at Judgement], he says [to his companion], "Oh, I wish there was between me and you the distance between the east and west - how wretched a companion."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But when such a person comes to Us, one will say ˹to their associate˺, “I wish you were as distant from me as the east is from the west! What an evil associate ˹you were˺!”

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَن يَنفَعَكُمُ ٱلۡيَوۡمَ إِذ ظَّلَمۡتُمۡ أَنَّكُمۡ فِي ٱلۡعَذَابِ مُشۡتَرِكُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walan yanfaAAakumu alyawma ith thalamtum annakum fee alAAathabi mushtarikoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And never will it benefit you that Day, when you have wronged, that you are [all] sharing in the punishment.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹It will be said to both,˺ “Since you all did wrong, sharing in the punishment will be of no benefit to you this Day.”

ayahs.textArabic: أَفَأَنتَ تُسۡمِعُ ٱلصُّمَّ أَوۡ تَهۡدِي ٱلۡعُمۡيَ وَمَن كَانَ فِي ضَلَٰلٖ مُّبِينٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Afaanta tusmiAAu alssumma aw tahdee alAAumya waman kana fee dalalin mubeenin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then will you make the deaf hear, [O Muhammad], or guide the blind or he who is in clear error?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Can you make the deaf hear, or guide the blind or those clearly astray?

ayahs.textArabic: فَإِمَّا نَذۡهَبَنَّ بِكَ فَإِنَّا مِنۡهُم مُّنتَقِمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faimma nathhabanna bika fainna minhum muntaqimoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And whether [or not] We take you away [in death], indeed, We will take retribution upon them.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Even if We take you away ˹from this world˺, We will surely inflict punishment upon them.

ayahs.textArabic: أَوۡ نُرِيَنَّكَ ٱلَّذِي وَعَدۡنَٰهُمۡ فَإِنَّا عَلَيۡهِم مُّقۡتَدِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Aw nuriyannaka allathee waAAadnahum fainna AAalayhim muqtadiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or whether [or not] We show you that which We have promised them, indeed, We are Perfect in Ability.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or if We show you what We threaten them with, We certainly have full power over them.

ayahs.textArabic: فَٱسۡتَمۡسِكۡ بِٱلَّذِيٓ أُوحِيَ إِلَيۡكَۖ إِنَّكَ عَلَىٰ صِرَٰطٖ مُّسۡتَقِيمٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faistamsik biallathee oohiya ilayka innaka AAala siratin mustaqeemin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So adhere to that which is revealed to you. Indeed, you are on a straight path.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So hold firmly to what has been revealed to you ˹O Prophet˺. You are truly on the Straight Path.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِنَّهُۥ لَذِكۡرٞ لَّكَ وَلِقَوۡمِكَۖ وَسَوۡفَ تُسۡـَٔلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wainnahu lathikrun laka waliqawmika wasawfa tusaloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, it is a remembrance for you and your people, and you [all] are going to be questioned.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely this ˹Quran˺ is a glory for you and your people. And you will ˹all˺ be questioned ˹about it˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَسۡـَٔلۡ مَنۡ أَرۡسَلۡنَا مِن قَبۡلِكَ مِن رُّسُلِنَآ أَجَعَلۡنَا مِن دُونِ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ ءَالِهَةٗ يُعۡبَدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waisal man arsalna min qablika min rusulina ajaAAalna min dooni alrrahmani alihatan yuAAbadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And ask those We sent before you of Our messengers; have We made besides the Most Merciful deities to be worshipped?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Ask ˹the followers of˺ the messengers that We already sent before you if We ˹ever˺ appointed ˹other˺ gods to be worshipped besides the Most Compassionate.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ أَرۡسَلۡنَا مُوسَىٰ بِـَٔايَٰتِنَآ إِلَىٰ فِرۡعَوۡنَ وَمَلَإِيْهِۦ فَقَالَ إِنِّي رَسُولُ رَبِّ ٱلۡعَٰلَمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad arsalna moosa biayatina ila firAAawna wamalaihi faqala innee rasoolu rabbi alAAalameena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And certainly did We send Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his establishment, and he said, "Indeed, I am the messenger of the Lord of the worlds."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, We sent Moses with Our signs to Pharaoh and his chiefs, and he said: “I am a messenger of the Lord of all worlds.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَلَمَّا جَآءَهُم بِـَٔايَٰتِنَآ إِذَا هُم مِّنۡهَا يَضۡحَكُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Falamma jaahum biayatina itha hum minha yadhakoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But when he brought them Our signs, at once they laughed at them.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But as soon as he came to them with Our signs, they laughed at them,

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا نُرِيهِم مِّنۡ ءَايَةٍ إِلَّا هِيَ أَكۡبَرُ مِنۡ أُخۡتِهَاۖ وَأَخَذۡنَٰهُم بِٱلۡعَذَابِ لَعَلَّهُمۡ يَرۡجِعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama nureehim min ayatin illa hiya akbaru min okhtiha waakhathnahum bialAAathabi laAAallahum yarjiAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We showed them not a sign except that it was greater than its sister, and We seized them with affliction that perhaps they might return [to faith].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: although every sign We showed them was greater than the one before. Ultimately, We seized them with torments so that they might return ˹to the Right Path˺.

ayahs.textArabic: وَقَالُواْ يَٰٓأَيُّهَ ٱلسَّاحِرُ ٱدۡعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ بِمَا عَهِدَ عِندَكَ إِنَّنَا لَمُهۡتَدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqaloo ya ayyuha alsahiru odAAu lana rabbaka bima AAahida AAindaka innana lamuhtadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they said [to Moses], "O magician, invoke for us your Lord by what He has promised you. Indeed, we will be guided."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Then˺ they pleaded, “O ˹mighty˺ magician! Pray to your Lord on our behalf, by virtue of the covenant He made with you. We will certainly accept guidance.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَلَمَّا كَشَفۡنَا عَنۡهُمُ ٱلۡعَذَابَ إِذَا هُمۡ يَنكُثُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Falamma kashafna AAanhumu alAAathaba itha hum yankuthoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But when We removed from them the affliction, at once they broke their word.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But as soon as We removed the torments from them, they broke their promise.

ayahs.textArabic: وَنَادَىٰ فِرۡعَوۡنُ فِي قَوۡمِهِۦ قَالَ يَٰقَوۡمِ أَلَيۡسَ لِي مُلۡكُ مِصۡرَ وَهَٰذِهِ ٱلۡأَنۡهَٰرُ تَجۡرِي مِن تَحۡتِيٓۚ أَفَلَا تُبۡصِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wanada firAAawnu fee qawmihi qala ya qawmi alaysa lee mulku misra wahathihi alanharu tajree min tahtee afala tubsiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And Pharaoh called out among his people; he said, "O my people, does not the kingdom of Egypt belong to me, and these rivers flowing beneath me; then do you not see?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And Pharaoh called out to his people, boasting, “O my people! Am I not sovereign over Egypt as well as ˹all˺ these streams flowing at my feet? Can you not see?

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ أَنَا۠ خَيۡرٞ مِّنۡ هَٰذَا ٱلَّذِي هُوَ مَهِينٞ وَلَا يَكَادُ يُبِينُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am ana khayrun min hatha allathee huwa maheenun wala yakadu yubeenu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or am I [not] better than this one who is insignificant and hardly makes himself clear?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Am I not better than this nobody who can hardly express himself?

ayahs.textArabic: فَلَوۡلَآ أُلۡقِيَ عَلَيۡهِ أَسۡوِرَةٞ مِّن ذَهَبٍ أَوۡ جَآءَ مَعَهُ ٱلۡمَلَٰٓئِكَةُ مُقۡتَرِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Falawla olqiya AAalayhi aswiratun min thahabin aw jaa maAAahu almalaikatu muqtarineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then why have there not been placed upon him bracelets of gold or come with him the angels in conjunction?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Why then have no golden bracelets ˹of kingship˺ been granted to him or angels come with him as escorts!”

ayahs.textArabic: فَٱسۡتَخَفَّ قَوۡمَهُۥ فَأَطَاعُوهُۚ إِنَّهُمۡ كَانُواْ قَوۡمٗا فَٰسِقِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faistakhaffa qawmahu faataAAoohu innahum kanoo qawman fasiqeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So he bluffed his people, and they obeyed him. Indeed, they were [themselves] a people defiantly disobedient [of Allah].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And so he fooled his people, and they obeyed him. They were truly a rebellious people.

ayahs.textArabic: فَلَمَّآ ءَاسَفُونَا ٱنتَقَمۡنَا مِنۡهُمۡ فَأَغۡرَقۡنَٰهُمۡ أَجۡمَعِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Falamma asafoona intaqamna minhum faaghraqnahum ajmaAAeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when they angered Us, We took retribution from them and drowned them all.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So when they enraged Us, We inflicted punishment upon them, drowning them all.

ayahs.textArabic: فَجَعَلۡنَٰهُمۡ سَلَفٗا وَمَثَلٗا لِّلۡأٓخِرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: FajaAAalnahum salafan wamathalan lilakhireena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We made them a precedent and an example for the later peoples.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We made them an example and a lesson for those after them.

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ وَلَمَّا ضُرِبَ ٱبۡنُ مَرۡيَمَ مَثَلًا إِذَا قَوۡمُكَ مِنۡهُ يَصِدُّونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walamma duriba ibnu maryama mathalan itha qawmuka minhu yasiddoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when the son of Mary was presented as an example, immediately your people laughed aloud.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: When the son of Mary was cited as an example ˹in argument˺, your people ˹O Prophet˺ broke into ˹joyful˺ applause.

ayahs.textArabic: وَقَالُوٓاْ ءَأَٰلِهَتُنَا خَيۡرٌ أَمۡ هُوَۚ مَا ضَرَبُوهُ لَكَ إِلَّا جَدَلَۢاۚ بَلۡ هُمۡ قَوۡمٌ خَصِمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqaloo aalihatuna khayrun am huwa ma daraboohu laka illa jadalan bal hum qawmun khasimoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they said, "Are your gods better, or is he?" They did not present the comparison except for [mere] argument. But, [in fact], they are a people prone to dispute.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They exclaimed, “Which is better: our gods or Jesus?” They cite him only to argue. In fact, they are a people prone to dispute.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنۡ هُوَ إِلَّا عَبۡدٌ أَنۡعَمۡنَا عَلَيۡهِ وَجَعَلۡنَٰهُ مَثَلٗا لِّبَنِيٓ إِسۡرَٰٓءِيلَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: In huwa illa AAabdun anAAamna AAalayhi wajaAAalnahu mathalan libanee israeela
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Jesus was not but a servant upon whom We bestowed favor, and We made him an example for the Children of Israel.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He was only a servant We showed favour to, and made as an example for the Children of Israel.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَوۡ نَشَآءُ لَجَعَلۡنَا مِنكُم مَّلَٰٓئِكَةٗ فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ يَخۡلُفُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaw nashao lajaAAalna minkum malaikatan fee alardi yakhlufoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And if We willed, We could have made [instead] of you angels succeeding [one another] on the earth.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Had We willed, We could have easily replaced you ˹all˺ with angels, succeeding one another on earth.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِنَّهُۥ لَعِلۡمٞ لِّلسَّاعَةِ فَلَا تَمۡتَرُنَّ بِهَا وَٱتَّبِعُونِۚ هَٰذَا صِرَٰطٞ مُّسۡتَقِيمٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wainnahu laAAilmun lilssaAAati fala tamtarunna biha waittabiAAooni hatha siratun mustaqeemun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, Jesus will be [a sign for] knowledge of the Hour, so be not in doubt of it, and follow Me. This is a straight path.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And his ˹second˺ coming is truly a sign for the Hour. So have no doubt about it, and follow me. This is the Straight Path.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَا يَصُدَّنَّكُمُ ٱلشَّيۡطَٰنُۖ إِنَّهُۥ لَكُمۡ عَدُوّٞ مُّبِينٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wala yasuddannakumu alshshaytanu innahu lakum AAaduwwun mubeenun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And never let Satan avert you. Indeed, he is to you a clear enemy.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And do not let Satan hinder you, ˹for˺ he is certainly your sworn enemy.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَمَّا جَآءَ عِيسَىٰ بِٱلۡبَيِّنَٰتِ قَالَ قَدۡ جِئۡتُكُم بِٱلۡحِكۡمَةِ وَلِأُبَيِّنَ لَكُم بَعۡضَ ٱلَّذِي تَخۡتَلِفُونَ فِيهِۖ فَٱتَّقُواْ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walamma jaa AAeesa bialbayyinati qala qad jitukum bialhikmati waliobayyina lakum baAAda allathee takhtalifoona feehi faittaqoo Allaha waateeAAooni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when Jesus brought clear proofs, he said, "I have come to you with wisdom and to make clear to you some of that over which you differ, so fear Allah and obey me.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: When Jesus came with clear proofs, he declared, “I have come to you with wisdom, and to clarify to you some of what you differ about. So fear Allah, and obey me.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ رَبِّي وَرَبُّكُمۡ فَٱعۡبُدُوهُۚ هَٰذَا صِرَٰطٞ مُّسۡتَقِيمٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna Allaha huwa rabbee warabbukum faoAAbudoohu hatha siratun mustaqeemun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. This is a straight path."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely Allah ˹alone˺ is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him ˹alone˺. This is the Straight Path.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَٱخۡتَلَفَ ٱلۡأَحۡزَابُ مِنۢ بَيۡنِهِمۡۖ فَوَيۡلٞ لِّلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُواْ مِنۡ عَذَابِ يَوۡمٍ أَلِيمٍ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faikhtalafa alahzabu min baynihim fawaylun lillatheena thalamoo min AAathabi yawmin aleemin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But the denominations from among them differed [and separated], so woe to those who have wronged from the punishment of a painful Day.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Yet their ˹various˺ groups have differed among themselves ˹about him˺, so woe to the wrongdoers when they face the torment of a painful Day!

ayahs.textArabic: هَلۡ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَّا ٱلسَّاعَةَ أَن تَأۡتِيَهُم بَغۡتَةٗ وَهُمۡ لَا يَشۡعُرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hal yanthuroona illa alssaAAata an tatiyahum baghtatan wahum la yashAAuroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Are they waiting except for the Hour to come upon them suddenly while they perceive not?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Are they waiting for the Hour to take them by surprise when they least expect ˹it˺?

ayahs.textArabic: ٱلۡأَخِلَّآءُ يَوۡمَئِذِۭ بَعۡضُهُمۡ لِبَعۡضٍ عَدُوٌّ إِلَّا ٱلۡمُتَّقِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Alakhillao yawmaithin baAAduhum libaAAdin AAaduwwun illa almuttaqeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Close friends, that Day, will be enemies to each other, except for the righteous
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Close friends will be enemies to one another on that Day, except the righteous,

ayahs.textArabic: يَٰعِبَادِ لَا خَوۡفٌ عَلَيۡكُمُ ٱلۡيَوۡمَ وَلَآ أَنتُمۡ تَحۡزَنُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ya AAibadi la khawfun AAalaykumu alyawma wala antum tahzanoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [To whom Allah will say], "O My servants, no fear will there be concerning you this Day, nor will you grieve,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹who will be told,˺ “O My servants! There is no fear for you Today, nor will you grieve—

ayahs.textArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ بِـَٔايَٰتِنَا وَكَانُواْ مُسۡلِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Allatheena amanoo biayatina wakanoo muslimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [You] who believed in Our verses and were Muslims.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹those˺ who believed in Our signs and ˹fully˺ submitted ˹to Us˺.

ayahs.textArabic: ٱدۡخُلُواْ ٱلۡجَنَّةَ أَنتُمۡ وَأَزۡوَٰجُكُمۡ تُحۡبَرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Odkhuloo aljannata antum waazwajukum tuhbaroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Enter Paradise, you and your kinds, delighted."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Enter Paradise, you and your spouses, rejoicing.”

ayahs.textArabic: يُطَافُ عَلَيۡهِم بِصِحَافٖ مِّن ذَهَبٖ وَأَكۡوَابٖۖ وَفِيهَا مَا تَشۡتَهِيهِ ٱلۡأَنفُسُ وَتَلَذُّ ٱلۡأَعۡيُنُۖ وَأَنتُمۡ فِيهَا خَٰلِدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yutafu AAalayhim bisihafin min thahabin waakwabin wafeeha ma tashtaheehi alanfusu watalaththu alaAAyunu waantum feeha khalidoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Circulated among them will be plates and vessels of gold. And therein is whatever the souls desire and [what] delights the eyes, and you will abide therein eternally.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Golden trays and cups will be passed around to them. There will be whatever the souls desire and the eyes delight in. And you will be there forever.

ayahs.textArabic: وَتِلۡكَ ٱلۡجَنَّةُ ٱلَّتِيٓ أُورِثۡتُمُوهَا بِمَا كُنتُمۡ تَعۡمَلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Watilka aljannatu allatee oorithtumooha bima kuntum taAAmaloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And that is Paradise which you are made to inherit for what you used to do.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: That is the Paradise which you will be awarded for what you used to do.

ayahs.textArabic: لَكُمۡ فِيهَا فَٰكِهَةٞ كَثِيرَةٞ مِّنۡهَا تَأۡكُلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Lakum feeha fakihatun katheeratun minha takuloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: For you therein is much fruit from which you will eat.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: There you will have abundant fruit to eat from.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّ ٱلۡمُجۡرِمِينَ فِي عَذَابِ جَهَنَّمَ خَٰلِدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna almujrimeena fee AAathabi jahannama khalidoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, the criminals will be in the punishment of Hell, abiding eternally.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, the wicked will be in the torment of Hell forever.

ayahs.textArabic: لَا يُفَتَّرُ عَنۡهُمۡ وَهُمۡ فِيهِ مُبۡلِسُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: La yufattaru AAanhum wahum feehi mublisoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: It will not be allowed to subside for them, and they, therein, are in despair.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It will never be lightened for them, and there they will be overwhelmed with despair.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا ظَلَمۡنَٰهُمۡ وَلَٰكِن كَانُواْ هُمُ ٱلظَّٰلِمِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama thalamnahum walakin kanoo humu alththalimeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We did not wrong them, but it was they who were the wrongdoers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We did not wrong them, but it was they who were the wrongdoers.

ayahs.textArabic: وَنَادَوۡاْ يَٰمَٰلِكُ لِيَقۡضِ عَلَيۡنَا رَبُّكَۖ قَالَ إِنَّكُم مَّٰكِثُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wanadaw ya maliku liyaqdi AAalayna rabbuka qala innakum makithoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they will call, "O Malik, let your Lord put an end to us!" He will say, "Indeed, you will remain."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will cry, “O Mâlik! Let your Lord finish us off.” He will answer, “You are definitely here to stay.”

ayahs.textArabic: لَقَدۡ جِئۡنَٰكُم بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكۡثَرَكُمۡ لِلۡحَقِّ كَٰرِهُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Laqad jinakum bialhaqqi walakinna aktharakum lilhaqqi karihoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: We had certainly brought you the truth, but most of you, to the truth, were averse.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We certainly brought the truth to you, but most of you were resentful of the truth.

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ أَبۡرَمُوٓاْ أَمۡرٗا فَإِنَّا مُبۡرِمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am abramoo amran fainna mubrimoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or have they devised [some] affair? But indeed, We are devising [a plan].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or have they mastered some ˹evil˺ plan? Then We ˹too˺ are surely planning.

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ يَحۡسَبُونَ أَنَّا لَا نَسۡمَعُ سِرَّهُمۡ وَنَجۡوَىٰهُمۚ بَلَىٰ وَرُسُلُنَا لَدَيۡهِمۡ يَكۡتُبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am yahsaboona anna la nasmaAAu sirrahum wanajwahum bala warusuluna ladayhim yaktuboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or do they think that We hear not their secrets and their private conversations? Yes, [We do], and Our messengers are with them recording.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or do they think that We do not hear their ˹evil˺ thoughts and secret talks? Yes ˹We do˺! And Our messenger-angels are in their presence, recording ˹it all˺.

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡ إِن كَانَ لِلرَّحۡمَٰنِ وَلَدٞ فَأَنَا۠ أَوَّلُ ٱلۡعَٰبِدِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul in kana lilrrahmani waladun faana awwalu alAAabideena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, [O Muhammad], "If the Most Merciful had a son, then I would be the first of [his] worshippers."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “If the Most Compassionate ˹really˺ had offspring, I would be the first worshipper.”

ayahs.textArabic: سُبۡحَٰنَ رَبِّ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ رَبِّ ٱلۡعَرۡشِ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Subhana rabbi alssamawati waalardi rabbi alAAarshi AAamma yasifoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Exalted is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, Lord of the Throne, above what they describe.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Glorified is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the Lord of the Throne, far above what they claim.

ayahs.textArabic: فَذَرۡهُمۡ يَخُوضُواْ وَيَلۡعَبُواْ حَتَّىٰ يُلَٰقُواْ يَوۡمَهُمُ ٱلَّذِي يُوعَدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fatharhum yakhoodoo wayalAAaboo hatta yulaqoo yawmahumu allathee yooAAadoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So leave them to converse vainly and amuse themselves until they meet their Day which they are promised.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So let them indulge ˹in falsehood˺ and amuse ˹themselves˺ until they face their Day, which they have been warned of.

ayahs.textArabic: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِي فِي ٱلسَّمَآءِ إِلَٰهٞ وَفِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ إِلَٰهٞۚ وَهُوَ ٱلۡحَكِيمُ ٱلۡعَلِيمُ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wahuwa allathee fee alssamai ilahun wafee alardi ilahun wahuwa alhakeemu alAAaleemu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And it is Allah who is [the only] deity in the heaven, and on the earth [the only] deity. And He is the Wise, the Knowing.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It is He Who is ˹the only˺ God in the heavens and ˹the only˺ God on the earth. For He is the All-Wise, All-Knowing.

ayahs.textArabic: وَتَبَارَكَ ٱلَّذِي لَهُۥ مُلۡكُ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَا وَعِندَهُۥ عِلۡمُ ٱلسَّاعَةِ وَإِلَيۡهِ تُرۡجَعُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Watabaraka allathee lahu mulku alssamawati waalardi wama baynahuma waAAindahu AAilmu alssaAAati wailayhi turjaAAoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And blessed is He to whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them and with whom is knowledge of the Hour and to whom you will be returned.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And Blessed is the One to Whom belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and everything in between! With Him ˹alone˺ is the knowledge of the Hour. And to Him you will ˹all˺ be returned.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَا يَمۡلِكُ ٱلَّذِينَ يَدۡعُونَ مِن دُونِهِ ٱلشَّفَٰعَةَ إِلَّا مَن شَهِدَ بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَهُمۡ يَعۡلَمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wala yamliku allatheena yadAAoona min doonihi alshshafaAAata illa man shahida bialhaqqi wahum yaAAlamoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those they invoke besides Him do not possess [power of] intercession; but only those who testify to the truth [can benefit], and they know.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹But˺ those ˹objects of worship˺ they invoke besides Him have no power to intercede, except those who testify to the truth knowingly.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَئِن سَأَلۡتَهُم مَّنۡ خَلَقَهُمۡ لَيَقُولُنَّ ٱللَّهُۖ فَأَنَّىٰ يُؤۡفَكُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walain saaltahum man khalaqahum layaqoolunna Allahu faanna yufakoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And if you asked them who created them, they would surely say, "Allah." So how are they deluded?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: If you ask them ˹O Prophet˺ who created them, they will certainly say, “Allah!” How can they then be deluded ˹from the truth˺?

ayahs.textArabic: وَقِيلِهِۦ يَٰرَبِّ إِنَّ هَٰٓؤُلَآءِ قَوۡمٞ لَّا يُؤۡمِنُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqeelihi yarabbi inna haolai qawmun la yuminoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [Allah acknowledges] his saying, "O my Lord, indeed these are a people who do not believe."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Allah is Aware of˺ the Prophet’s cry: “O my Lord! Indeed, these are a people who persist in disbelief.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَٱصۡفَحۡ عَنۡهُمۡ وَقُلۡ سَلَٰمٞۚ فَسَوۡفَ يَعۡلَمُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faisfah AAanhum waqul salamun fasawfa yaAAlamoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So turn aside from them and say, "Peace." But they are going to know.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So bear with them and respond with peace. They will soon come to know.
